title: Tender Korean Tamarind Gimbap
description: A rich, buttery, and molten Korean dish from Jeju, featuring the sweetness of tamarind pulp, braised to perfection in a Buddhist-temple-friendly recipe.
instructions: Combine tamarind pulp and gochujang in a small saucepan. Heat over medium heat, whisking until smooth.
Add short-grain rice to the saucepan and cook, stirring constantly, until lightly toasted.
Transfer the rice mixture to a large bowl and let cool to room temperature.
In a separate pan, heat sesame oil over medium heat. Add onion and garlic and cook until softened.
Add zucchini and carrot to the pan and cook until tender.
In a separate pan, melt butter over medium heat. Add the cooked rice mixture and stir-fry until combined.
Transfer the rice mixture to a serving dish and top with the vegetable mixture and green onion.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional green onion if desired.
